assimilation: The act of assimilating or the state of being assimilated.
verdict: A decision on an issue of fact in a civil or criminal case or an inquest.
apple: A common, round fruit produced by the tree Malus domestica, cultivated in temperate climates.
socialism: Any of various economic and political theories advocating collective or governmental ownership and administration of the means of production and distribution of goods.
terrible: Dreadful; causing terror, alarm and fear; awesome
toxic: Having a chemical nature that is harmful to health or lethal if consumed or otherwise entering into the body in sufficient quantities.
selfish: Holding one's own self-interest as the standard for decision making.
shroud: That which clothes, covers, conceals, or protects; a garment.
conceal: To hide something from view or from public knowledge, to try to keep something secret.
squiggle: A short twisting or wiggling line or mark
contamination: The act or process of contaminating
contaminating: To make something dangerous or toxic by introducing impurities or foreign matter.
contaminate: To make something dangerous or toxic by introducing impurities or foreign matter.
defence: The action of defending, of protecting from attack, danger or injury.
swung: To rotate about an off-centre fixed point.
tendency: A likelihood of behaving in a particular way or going in a particular direction; a tending toward.
forth: Forward in time, place or degree.
